content: Half-life is calculated from the terminal elimination phase of the concentration-time curve: t1/2 = 0.693 / ke (elimination rate constant). Determined by plotting log concentration vs time and measuring slope of terminal phase. Context-sensitive half-life applies to IV infusions: time for 50% decrease after stopping infusion, which increases with infusion duration for drugs with multi-compartment kinetics (e.g., fentanyl, propofol). Effective half-life may differ from elimination half-life for drugs with active metabolites or non-linear kinetics. Zero-order kinetics (constant amount eliminated per time, not fraction) occurs with saturable metabolism (phenytoin, ethanol) where half-life increases with concentration.
